title: Postmenopausal Osteoporosis
chief_complaint: I fell from standing height and broke my wrist. My doctor said I have 'thin bones'. My mother had a hip fracture and lost 4 inches of height.

physical_exam.Musculoskeletal: Wrist in cast. Mild thoracic kyphosis. No vertebral tenderness.

hidden_data.dexa: Lumbar spine T-score -3.2. Femoral neck T-score -2.9. Total hip T-score -2.7. Osteoporosis at all sites.
hidden_data.xray_spine: Compression fractures T11, T12, L1 (old, asymptomatic)

correct_diagnosis: Postmenopausal Osteoporosis with Prevalent Vertebral Fractures and Vitamin D Deficiency
key_learning_points: Osteoporosis = T-score ≤-2.5 on DEXA. Osteopenia = T-score -1.0 to -2.4
FRAX tool estimates 10-year fracture risk. Treat if: Hip fracture in parent, previous fracture, FRAX high risk, or T-score ≤-2.5
First-line: Bisphosphonates (Alendronate 70mg weekly). Vitamin D 800-2000 IU + Calcium 1200mg daily
Prevalent vertebral fracture (even asymptomatic) = very high risk. Consider anabolic therapy (Teriparatide, Romosozumab) before antiresorptive
Duration: Bisphosphonates 3-5 years then drug holiday if low risk. Denosumab lifelong (rebound fractures if stopped)
